taxonomy: Chthamalia schaffneri is classified within the family Corallinaceae, a group of marine algae that includes red algae. It belongs to the order Corallinales, which is a subgroup of Corallinaceae. The family Corallinaceae is further classified within the class Florideophyceae, which is a group of red algae that are characterized by having a single cell layer. intro: Chthamalia schaffneri, also known as Mexican prickle poppy or Schaffner's prickly poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its prickly stems and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are known to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and immune system support.
